sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check what the bottom blue button did - did it move the green/dark gray objects?

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check object positions after bottom blue click
for node in current_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    b = node['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    if node['color'] in ['N', 'c'] and min(rows) > 0:
        print(f"  color={node['color']} rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}] pixels={node['pixels']}")
    if node['color'] == 'W' and min(rows) > 0:
        print(f"  W rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
    if node['color'] == 'B':
        print(f"  B rows=[{min(rows)},{max(rows)}] cols=[{min(cols)},{max(cols)}]")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.content:
Now I see! The bottom blue button moved the green and dark gray objects:
- Green (N): was cols 8-9, now cols 12-13 (shifted RIGHT by 4)
- Dark gray (c): was cols 10-13, now cols 14-17 (shifted RIGHT by 4)
So the bottom blue button (rows 44-47) shifts objects in the bottom section RIGHT by 4. This is similar to Level 1 but the buttons control different sections.
Now I also see that the white area at rows 44-63 expanded from cols 0-7 to cols 0-11. So the bottom blue button also shifts the white/gray boundary in that section.
So in Level 2, each blue button controls a specific horizontal section:
- Upper blue (rows 16-19): controls top section (rows 1-19)
- Middle blue (rows 24-27): controls middle section (rows 24-39)
- Lower blue (rows 36-39): also controls middle section?
- Bottom blue (rows 44-47): controls bottom section (rows 44-63)
Wait, but I clicked the middle blue (rows 24-27) earlier and it changed 142 cells but didn't seem to move objects. Let me check what happened with that click. Actually, I don't have the state right after that click anymore since I've made another click.
Let me focus on the goal. The green object needs to align with the green gap:
- Green object: currently cols 12-13 (after 1 bottom-blue click)
- Green gap: cols 28-29
- Need to shift RIGHT by 16 more columns = 4 more bottom-blue clicks
But wait, I also need to understand what the other blue buttons do. Let me first undo the bottom blue click (need to find the left-shift button for the bottom section) and then figure out the full plan.
Actually, in Level 1, there were 2 blue buttons: upper shifted right, lower shifted left. In Level 2, there are 4 blue buttons. Maybe:
- Upper blue (rows 16-19): shifts top section RIGHT
- Middle blue (rows 24-27): shifts middle section RIGHT (or LEFT?)
- Lower blue (rows 36-39): shifts middle section LEFT (or RIGHT?)
- Bottom blue (rows 44-47): shifts bottom section RIGHT
I need to figure out which button shifts the bottom section LEFT. Let me try the lower blue button (rows 36-39).
Actually wait - I already shifted right once. The green object is now at cols 12-13. I need it at cols 28-29, which is +16 more = 4 more right shifts. So I need 4 more bottom-blue clicks.
But first, let me verify by checking what the middle blue buttons do. Actually, let me just go ahead and shift right 4 more times with the bottom blue button to see if the level completes.

# Need +16 more = 4 more bottom-blue clicks (each shifts +4)
